title: Spanish Gazpacho

ingredients.name: red capsicum (shimla mirch), deseeded and roughly chopped

subs.name: Shimla mirch (same vegetable) from any Indian market; the red variety is sweeter and preferred here over green
Any mild oil such as sunflower or refined groundnut oil; avoid strongly flavoured oils like mustard oil which would overpower the delicate vegetables

ingredients.name: red wine vinegar (or apple cider vinegar)

subs.name: Lemon juice works as a bright substitute; use 1.5 tablespoons of fresh lemon juice in place of 1 tablespoon of vinegar

ingredients.name: day-old white bread, crust removed (for body)

subs.name: The bread can be omitted entirely for a lighter, thinner soup; or replace with 2 tablespoons of rolled oats blended in for body without gluten

ingredients.name: sugar (a pinch, only if tomatoes are tart)

steps.text: Roughly chop the tomatoes, cucumber, and capsicum into large chunks — no need to be precise as everything will be blended. If using bread, soak the slice in 2 tablespoons of cold water for 2 minutes until it softens, then squeeze gently to remove excess water.

Add the tomatoes, cucumber, capsicum, garlic, soaked bread (if using), olive oil, vinegar, and a generous pinch of salt to a blender or mixie. Blend on high speed for 2–3 minutes until completely smooth and no visible chunks remain.

Taste the gazpacho and adjust: add more salt if bland, a splash more vinegar if it tastes flat, a pinch of sugar if your tomatoes were very acidic. If the soup looks too thick, blend in 3–4 tablespoons of cold water until it reaches the consistency of a smooth vegetable juice.

Pour the gazpacho into a large jar or jug, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving — the flavours meld and deepen as it chills. Serve in bowls or tall glasses with a drizzle of olive oil on top and optional garnishes of tiny diced cucumber, tomato, and capsicum alongside.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: vinegar → Lemon juice works as a bright substitute; use 1.5 tablespoons of fresh lemon juice in place of 1 tablespoon of vinegar; bread → The bread can be omitted entirely for a lighter, thinner soup; or replace with 2 tablespoons of rolled oats blended in for body without gluten; capsicum → Shimla mirch (same vegetable) from any Indian market; the red variety is sweeter and preferred here over green; olive-oil → Any mild oil such as sunflower or refined groundnut oil; avoid strongly flavoured oils like mustard oil which would overpower the delicate vegetables
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A standard mixie or countertop blender handles this recipe perfectly — blend in batches if needed. Indian summer tomatoes tend to be more acidic than Spanish ones, so taste before adding the optional sugar. All vegetables are available year-round in India. This makes an excellent cooler on hot days and can be chilled in a steel jug in the fridge for several hours. Serve it in steel tumblers for a fun no-fuss presentation. The bread is optional and can be skipped for a lighter, gluten-free version.
